animals.title: African Elephant
animals.habitat: Savannas, forests, and grasslands of sub-Saharan Africa
animals.description: The African elephant is the largest land animal on Earth. They live in family groups led by an older female and can eat up to 300 pounds of food in a single day.

animals.title: Bengal Tiger
animals.habitat: Tropical and subtropical forests of India, Bangladesh, and Nepal
animals.description: The Bengal tiger is the most common subspecies of tiger. They are solitary hunters with orange coats and black stripes that help them blend into tall grasses.

animals.title: Polar Bear
animals.habitat: Sea ice and coastal regions of the Arctic Circle
animals.description: Polar bears are excellent swimmers and have thick layers of fat and fur to stay warm in freezing temperatures. Their main food source is seals.

animals.title: Giant Panda
animals.habitat: Bamboo forests in the mountains of central China
animals.description: Giant pandas spend most of their day eating bamboo. Even though they are classified as carnivores, about 99 percent of their diet is plants.

animals.title: Red Kangaroo
animals.habitat: Open plains and deserts across mainland Australia
animals.description: The red kangaroo is the largest marsupial in the world. They can hop at speeds of up to 35 miles per hour and cover 25 feet in a single jump.

animals.title: Emperor Penguin
animals.habitat: Ice shelves and cold waters of Antarctica
animals.description: Emperor penguins are the tallest penguin species. The males huddle together to keep eggs warm during the harsh Antarctic winter while the females hunt for food.
